states.name: Off
states.description: Device is off. This includes ordinary off, off due to Off/OffWithEffect/Toggle/OnWithTimedOff, and off after timers expire. OnOff == FALSE.

states.name: On
states.description: Device is on. This includes ordinary on, on due to On/Toggle/OnWithTimedOff, and on while any timers are active. OnOff == TRUE.

transitions.timing_requirements: Server responds with UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND; no attribute changes.
Server responds with UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND; no attribute changes.
Turn on when Lighting timers are not both supported; no OffWaitTime rule applies.
Lighting rule: when turning on and OnTime is 0, OffWaitTime is cleared.
Turn on; OffWaitTime not forced to 0 because OnTime is non-zero.
Idempotent turn-on when already on without both timers.
Idempotent turn-on while clearing OffWaitTime because OnTime is 0.
Idempotent turn-on; timers unchanged.
Server responds with UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND; no attribute changes.
Server responds with UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND; no attribute changes.
Simple toggle from off to on when Lighting timers are not both supported.
Toggle from off to on when both timers are supported: new OnOff == TRUE implies OnTime is set to 0 per spec.
Simple toggle from on to off when Lighting timers are not both supported.
Toggle from on to off with timers: after toggling, OnOff == FALSE and OnTime == 0 so OffWaitTime is cleared per spec.
Stores global scene, clears GlobalSceneControl and ensures device remains off; OnTime not supported so unchanged.
Stores global scene, clears GlobalSceneControl, ensures device off and clears OnTime.
Stores global scene, clears GlobalSceneControl and turns device off; OnTime not supported.
Stores global scene, clears GlobalSceneControl, turns device off and clears OnTime.
Just turns device off with effect; no scene stored and no OnTime attribute.
Turns device off with effect and clears OnTime; no scene stored.
Turns device off with effect; no scene stored and OnTime not supported.
Turns device off with effect and clears OnTime; no scene stored.
No effect when GlobalSceneControl attribute is not implemented by the server.
No effect when GlobalSceneControl attribute is not implemented by the server.
Command discarded when GlobalSceneControl is TRUE; no changes.
Command discarded when GlobalSceneControl is TRUE; no changes.
Recalls global scene and sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE; OnOff is updated by Scenes cluster or equivalent logic; Lighting timers not both present so no extra rule.

transitions.guard_condition: supports(GlobalSceneControl) && GlobalSceneControl == FALSE && supports(OnTime) && supports(OffWaitTime) && OnTime == 0
transitions.timing_requirements: Recalls global scene, sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE and clears OffWaitTime because OnTime is 0 after recall.

transitions.guard_condition: supports(GlobalSceneControl) && GlobalSceneControl == FALSE && supports(OnTime) && supports(OffWaitTime) && OnTime != 0
transitions.timing_requirements: Recalls global scene and sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE; OffWaitTime unchanged because OnTime is non-zero after recall.
Idempotent recall of global scene and setting GlobalSceneControl TRUE when already on without both timers.

transitions.guard_condition: supports(GlobalSceneControl) && GlobalSceneControl == FALSE && supports(OnTime) && supports(OffWaitTime) && OnTime == 0
transitions.timing_requirements: Recalls scene, sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE and clears OffWaitTime because OnTime is 0 after recall.

transitions.guard_condition: supports(GlobalSceneControl) && GlobalSceneControl == FALSE && supports(OnTime) && supports(OffWaitTime) && OnTime != 0
transitions.timing_requirements: Recalls scene, sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE; OffWaitTime unchanged because OnTime is non-zero.
Command discarded when AcceptOnlyWhenOn is set and device is off.
Guarded-off case: adjusts OffWaitTime while staying off; matches effect_on_receipt branch when OffWaitTime > 0 and OnOff is FALSE.
When not guarded by OffWaitTime, command turns device on and sets timers.
On-side branch: device remains on and timers are updated.
Periodic decrement while device is on and timers are finite: OnTime reduced every 0.1 s.
When OnTime has reached 0 under finite semantics, OffWaitTime set to 0 and device turns off; no further periodic updates occur because OffWaitTime == 0.
Periodic decrement while off and timers are finite: OffWaitTime reduced every 0.1 s.
When OffWaitTime reaches 0 under finite semantics, periodic updates stop; device remains off.
No automatic decrement when either timer is disabled by 0xFFFF.
No automatic decrement when either timer is disabled by 0xFFFF.
Startup behavior: force off.
Startup behavior: force on.
Startup behavior: toggle from previously off to on.
Startup behavior: toggle from previously on to off.

scene_behaviors: OffWithEffect: if GlobalSceneControl == TRUE, the server stores the current global scene via store_global_scene(), sets GlobalSceneControl to FALSE, then turns the device off and, when OnTime is supported, sets OnTime to 0. If GlobalSceneControl == FALSE or not supported, it simply turns the device off (and clears OnTime if supported).

OnWithRecallGlobalScene: if GlobalSceneControl == FALSE, the server invokes recall_global_scene() to restore the previously stored global scene (or equivalent restoration logic), then sets GlobalSceneControl to TRUE. After this, if OnTime and OffWaitTime are supported and OnTime == 0, OffWaitTime is set to 0.
The OnOff attribute is scene-capable and may be stored and recalled as part of scenes, including the special global scene with group ID 0 and scene ID 0.

definitions.definition: Boolean attribute indicating whether the device is logically off (FALSE) or on (TRUE). It is controlled by On, Off, Toggle, OffWithEffect, OnWithRecallGlobalScene via scenes, and OnWithTimedOff, and it also participates in feature behaviors such as DeadFrontBehavior.
definitions.usage_context: Used in state invariants and as a guard for commands such as OnWithTimedOff when AcceptOnlyWhenOn is set.

definitions.definition: Unsigned 16-bit attribute, in 1/10 second units, representing how long the device should remain on under OnWithTimedOff control. A value of 0 means no remaining timed-on duration; a value of 0xFFFF disables automatic decrement by the periodic timer.
definitions.usage_context: Updated by OnWithTimedOff when the device is on or being turned on; decremented on periodic ticks when timers are finite and OnOff is TRUE; cleared by Off and certain Lighting-feature interactions.

definitions.definition: Unsigned 16-bit attribute, in 1/10 second units, representing a guard interval related to the off state for OnWithTimedOff. A value of 0 means no remaining guard interval; a value of 0xFFFF disables automatic decrement by the periodic timer.
definitions.usage_context: Adjusted by OnWithTimedOff when the device is off in the guarded-off case or when turning on; decremented on periodic ticks when timers are finite and OnOff is FALSE; cleared in Lighting rules when turning on with OnTime == 0 or when OnTime reaches 0.

definitions.definition: Boolean Lighting attribute controlling global scene behavior. When TRUE, the next OffWithEffect stores the current settings as the global scene and then sets this attribute to FALSE. When FALSE, OnWithRecallGlobalScene may recall that stored global scene and then sets this attribute to TRUE.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards and actions for OffWithEffect and OnWithRecallGlobalScene transitions.

definitions.definition: Bitmap attribute that encodes supported features of the On/Off cluster, including LT (Lighting), DF (DeadFrontBehavior), and OFFONLY (OffOnly). Bits not described here are out of scope of this model.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards that block On and Toggle when OFFONLY is set and to imply support of Lighting-related attributes and commands when LT is present.

definitions.definition: Feature indicating that the server supports only the Off command; On and Toggle commands are not supported and must return an UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND error.
definitions.usage_context: Checked in guards for On and Toggle transitions to prevent any attribute changes when OFFONLY is set.

definitions.definition: Bitmap type used by the OnWithTimedOff command. It includes a bit AcceptOnlyWhenOn that, when set, requires the OnOff attribute to be TRUE for the command to be accepted.
definitions.usage_context: Used in OnWithTimedOff transitions to discard the command when AcceptOnlyWhenOn is set and the device is off.

definitions.definition: Enumeration controlling OnOff behavior at startup. Values include Off, On, and Toggle, determining whether the device comes up off, on, or toggles relative to its stored previous OnOff state.
definitions.usage_context: Used in power_up_event transitions to set OnOff according to configured startup behavior.

definitions.definition: Abstract operation that saves the current global scene, including the OnOff attribute and any related lighting parameters, into the Scenes Management cluster or equivalent storage under a reserved global identifier.
definitions.usage_context: Invoked in OffWithEffect transitions when GlobalSceneControl is TRUE before turning the device off.

definitions.definition: Abstract operation that restores a previously stored global scene, including the OnOff attribute and possibly other attributes, via the Scenes Management cluster or equivalent logic.
definitions.usage_context: Invoked in OnWithRecallGlobalScene transitions when GlobalSceneControl is FALSE to restore the global scene.

definitions.definition: Abstract periodic event representing a 0.1 second interval, used to decrement OnTime or OffWaitTime under OnWithTimedOff semantics when both timers are finite.
definitions.usage_context: Used as the trigger for timer-decrement transitions implementing the periodic behavior described for OnWithTimedOff.

references.name: On/Off cluster
references.description: Primary specification for On/Off behavior including attributes, commands, and Lighting feature semantics modeled by this FSM.

references.name: Level Control cluster
references.description: Referenced cluster whose commands can affect OnOff, OnTime, and OffWaitTime, especially when the Lighting feature is present.

references.name: Scenes Management server cluster
references.description: Provides global scene storage and recall used by OffWithEffect and OnWithRecallGlobalScene.

references.name: UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND status
references.description: Interaction Model error returned when clients invoke unsupported commands such as On or Toggle when OFFONLY is set.

references.name: INVALID_IN_STATE status
references.description: Status used by other clusters when DF (DeadFrontBehavior) feature causes temporary rejection of operations while the device is off.

references.name: Data Model Specification
references.description: Defines base types like bool, uint16, enum8, and map8, and generic attribute semantics referenced here.

security_properties.description: When OFFONLY feature is present, On and Toggle commands must never change OnOff or timer attributes.
security_properties.formal_specification: For all transitions with trigger in {On command, Toggle command}, if (FeatureMap & OFFONLY) != 0 then OnOff_after == OnOff_before && OnTime_after == OnTime_before && OffWaitTime_after == OffWaitTime_before.
security_properties.attack_vector: A client may attempt to bypass regulatory limits by sending On or Toggle commands to a device that should only be turned off.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM guards ensure that when OFFONLY is set, On and Toggle transitions have empty action lists, so no attribute is modified.

security_properties.description: OnWithTimedOff obeys the specified guarded-off and timer semantics, including guarded-off behavior when OffWaitTime > 0 and OnOff is FALSE, and periodic timer decrements when timers are finite.
security_properties.formal_specification: If a transition with trigger OnWithTimedOff occurs with OnOff_before == FALSE and OffWaitTime_before > 0 and OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 0, then OffWaitTime_after == min(OffWaitTime_before, OffWaitTimeField) and OnOff_after == FALSE. If OnOff_before == FALSE, OffWaitTime_before <= 0 and OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 0, then OnOff_after == TRUE and OnTime_after == max(OnTime_before, OnTimeField) and OffWaitTime_after == OffWaitTimeField. For periodic ticks when OnTime != 0xFFFF && OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF, while OnOff == TRUE and OnTime > 0, OnTime strictly decreases; when OnTime reaches 0, OffWaitTime is set to 0 and OnOff becomes FALSE; while OnOff == FALSE and OffWaitTime > 0, OffWaitTime strictly decreases until 0.
security_properties.attack_vector: Incorrect timer handling could allow clients to hold the device on or off beyond intended durations, or to bypass guard intervals.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM transitions directly encode the provided effect_on_receipt and state descriptions, including guarded-off condition and periodic decrement behaviors.

security_properties.description: Toggle command enforces the Lighting rules for timers: when toggling on with timers supported, it sets OnTime to 0; when toggling off with both timers supported, it clears both OnTime and OffWaitTime, ensuring consistency.
security_properties.formal_specification: For any Toggle transition where supports(OnTime) && supports(OffWaitTime) and OnOff_before == FALSE, we have OnOff_after == TRUE && OnTime_after == 0. For any Toggle transition where supports(OnTime) && supports(OffWaitTime) and OnOff_before == TRUE, we have OnOff_after == FALSE && OnTime_after == 0 && OffWaitTime_after == 0.
security_properties.attack_vector: An attacker could try to create inconsistent timer states by repeatedly toggling, leaving stale OffWaitTime or non-zero OnTime values that conflict with the Lighting semantics.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM actions for Toggle explicitly set timers in these cases and preserve state invariants.

security_properties.description: OnWithRecallGlobalScene always applies the Lighting rule that when timers are supported and OnTime is 0 after recall, OffWaitTime must be 0.
security_properties.formal_specification: For any OnWithRecallGlobalScene transition where supports(OnTime) && supports(OffWaitTime) and GlobalSceneControl_before == FALSE, if OnTime_after == 0 then OffWaitTime_after == 0.
security_properties.attack_vector: A client could leverage scene recall to leave OffWaitTime non-zero while OnTime is 0, causing inconsistent timing behavior and unexpected guarded-off periods.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM transitions for OnWithRecallGlobalScene include branches that set OffWaitTime to 0 whenever OnTime is 0 after executing recall_global_scene().

security_properties.description: On power up, OnOff is determined only by StartUpOnOff and the stored previous OnOff value in the Toggle case.
security_properties.formal_specification: For all power_up_event transitions: if StartUpOnOff == Off then OnOff_after == FALSE; if StartUpOnOff == On then OnOff_after == TRUE; if StartUpOnOff == Toggle then OnOff_after == not(previous_OnOff).
security_properties.attack_vector: Repeated power cycling might be used to drive the device into undefined or unsafe states if startup rules are not deterministic or depend on uncontrolled variables.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM contains explicit power_up_event transitions mapping each StartUpOnOffEnum value to a well-defined new OnOff state based only on the previous OnOff value.
